subject: Ancient History
topic: Art, Literature And Culture During Ancient Times
tags: Art, Literature And Culture During Ancient Times
stem: Who among the following was not a contemporary of the other three?

solution.detail:
Milinda’s period is separated by centuries with other given personalities.
- Bimbisara (approx. 558–491 BCE) was the ruler of Magadha and a contemporary of Gautama Buddha (c. 563–483 BCE).
- Prasenjit (6th century BCE) was the king of Kosala and also lived during the time of Buddha.
- Milinda (Greek king Menander I) ruled much later, in the 2nd century BCE. He was not a contemporary of the other three, making him the correct answer.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. The Headquarters of the International Organisation for standardization are located in Rome. 2. ISO 9000 relates to the quality management system and standards. 3. ISO 14000 relates to environmental management system standards. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Headquarters of the International Organization for Standardization (ISO) is located in Geneva, Switzerland. ISO (International Organization for Standardization) is a non governmental global body that develops standards to ensure quality, safety, and efficiency in products, services, and systems. Established in 1946, it facilitates international trade and cooperation by harmonizing practices worldwide, making lives easier, safer, and better. Statement 2 is correct: ISO 9000 is a series of standards related to quality management systems, ensuring organizations meet customer and regulatory requirements. Statement 3 is correct: ISO 14000 is a series of stand-ards related to environmental management systems, helping organizations minimize environmental impact.

stem: Consider the following statements: 1. The Constitution of the United States of America came into force in year 1810. 2. All revenue bills must originate in the House of Representative of the US Congress. 3. George W. Bush is the only President in the history of the United States of America. Whose father was also the President of the United States of America. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Constitution of the United States came into force on March 4, 1789. Statement 2 is correct: All revenue bills must originate in the House of Representatives of the US Congress. This is stated in Article I, Section 7, Clause 1 of the Constitution, also known as the Origination Clause or Revenue Clause. Statement 3 is incorrect: George W. Bush is not the only President in the history of the United States whose father was also President. His father, George H. W. Bush was the 41st President. John Adams, the second President of the United States, and his son John Quincy Adams, the sixth President, both held the post of the president of the USA.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. The Charter of the United Nations Organization was adopted at Geneva, Switzerland in June, 1945. 2. India was admitted to the United Nations Organization in the year 1945. 3. The Trusteeship Council of the United Nations Organization was established to manage the affairs of territories detached from Japan Italy after the second World War or such territories not under the control of a country at that time. Which of the statements given above is/are correct? .

solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: Charter of the United Nations was adopted in San Francisco, California, USA, on June 26, 1945. Statement 2 is correct: India was admitted to the United Nations Organization in 1945, becoming one of the original member states. Statement 3 is correct: The Trusteeship Council was established to oversee the administration of trust territories, which were former colonies or territories detached from nations like Japan and Italy after World War II. The Trusteeship Council is made up of the five permanent members of the Security Council → China, France, the Russian Federation, the United Kingdom and the United States.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: In which country is Bandung, where the conference of African and Asian nations was held which led to establishing Non-Aligned Movement (NAM) situated?

solution.detail: Bandung is a city in Indonesia, where the historic Bandung Conference was held in 1955. This conference brought together leaders from African and Asian nations to promote economic and cultural cooperation and oppose colonialism or neocolonialism by any nation. It laid the foundation for the establishment of the Non-Aligned Movement (NAM) in 1961. Sukarno (Indonesia), Jawaharlal Nehru (India), Gamal Abdel Nasser (Egypt), Kwame Nkrumah (Ghana) and Ho Chi Minh (Vietnam) aware its main leaders along with few others.The XIX Summit of the Non-Aligned Movement was held on January 15-20, 2024 in Kampala.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: In which one of the following countries, is Tamil a major language?

solution.detail: Tamil is a major language in Singapore. It is one of the four official languages of Singapore, alongside English, Malay, and Mandarin. The Tamil-speaking community in Singapore is significant, and Tamil is used in education, media, and government. Malaya in roman script is Singapore’s National Language.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: For which one of the following countries is Spanish not an official language?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Republic of Congo
solution.detail: The official language of the Republic of Congo is French. It has four national languages: Swahili, Tshiluba (Kiluba), Lingala, and Kongo. French is the official language and the language of instruction, business, administration, and international communications. The Republic of Congo, like many countries in Africa, was colonized by European powers. Specifically, it was a French colony, and as a result, French became the official language. Thus, Spanish is not an official language in the Republic of Congo due to its colonial history with France.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: What was Komagata Maru?
options: A political party based in Taiwan.
A naval ship on voyage to Canada.
A Chinese village where Mao Tse Lung began his long march.

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: A naval ship on voyage to Canada.
solution.detail: The Komagata Maru was a Japanese steamship that gained historical significance due to an incident in 1914 involving Indian immigrants attempting to immigrate to Canada. The ship carried 376 passengers, mostly Sikhs, Punjabis, and other Indians, who were seeking a new life in Canada. Upon arrival in Vancouver, the passengers were denied entry due to Canada’s discriminatory Continuous Journey Regulation, which aimed to restrict immigration. After being stranded for two months, the ship was forced to return to India. Upon arrival in Calcutta, the passengers faced arrest and violence from British colonial authorities.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Which one of the following cities is the seat of Parliament of South Africa?

solution.detail: The Parliament of South Africa is located in Cape Town. While South Africa has three capital cities, each serving a different purpose, Cape Town is the legislative capital and houses the Parliament. Pretoria is the Administrative capital (seat of the executive branch). Cape Town is the Legislative capital (seat of the Parliament). Bloemfontein is the Judicial capital (seat of the Supreme Court of Appeal).

subject: Current Affairs

The storm’s impact was particularly severe in Gonaïves, Haiti’s third-largest city, where a 9-foot wall of water inundated the area, leading to widespread destruction.

subject: Current Affairs

(BSNL) was incorporated in September, 2000 under the Indian Companies Act, 1956 with the objective to take over the business of providing telecom services and network of the erstwhile Department of Communications with effect from 1st October, 2000. Before that, telecom services were directly managed by the Department of Telecommunications. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) was, thus, established with effect from 20th February 1997 by an Act of Parliament, called the Telecom Regulatory Authority of India Act, 1997, to regulate telecom services, including fixation/revision of tariffs for telecom services which were earlier vested in the Central Government.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Which one of the following pairs is not correctly matched?
options: Arjun : Indigenously produced Main Battle Tank (MBT)

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Phalcon : Cruise missile supplied by Russia to India.
solution.detail:
Pair (b) is incorrectly matched: Phalcon is not a cruise missile. It is an Airborne Early Warning and Control System (AWACS) developed by Israel in collaboration with India. Pair (a), (c) and (d) are correctly matched:

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Who among the following is not a recipient of the Bharat Ratna Award?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Raj Kapoor
solution.detail: Raj Kapoor is widely regarded as one of the greatest icons of Indian cinema. He was honored with the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1987 for his outstanding contribution to Indian films. However, he did not receive the Bharat Ratna.
solution.extraEdge:
Ustad Bismillah Khan received Bharat Ratna in 2001, Satyajit ray in 1992 and Lata Mangeshkar in 2001. About Bharat Ratna:
- Instituted in 1954, the Bharat Ratna is India’s highest civilian award, conferred on the recommendation of the Prime Minister to the President without formal nominations. A maximum of three awards can be given annually, with exceptions in 1999 and 2024, when four and five were awarded respectively. The award consists of a peepal leaf-shaped medallion, inscribed with “Bharat Ratna” on the front and the State Emblem of India with “Satyameva Jayate” on the reverse, along with a certificate (sanad). It is minted at the Alipore Mint, Kolkata. The first recipients in 1954 were C. Rajagopalachari, Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, and C. V. Raman.
- Initially, posthumous awards were not allowed, but this changed in 1966, with Lal Bahadur Shastri becoming the first posthumous recipient. The award is generally reserved for Indian citizens, but exceptions have been made for Mother Teresa, Abdul Ghaffar Khan, and Nelson Mandela in recognition of their contributions to humanity.

stem: Which one of the following statements is not correct?
options: Rourkela Steel Plant, the first integrated steel plant in the Public Sector of India was set up with the Soviet Union collaboration.
Salem Steel Plant is a premier producer of stainless steel in India.
Maharashtra Elektrosmelt Ltd. is a subsidiary of the Steel Authority of India Ltd.
Visakhapatnam Steel Plant is a unit of the Rashtriya Ispat Nigam Ltd.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Rourkela Steel Plant, the first integrated steel plant in the Public Sector of India was set up with the Soviet Union collaboration.
solution.detail:
Rourkela Steel Plant was established with West German collaboration, not the Soviet Union. It was India’s first public sector integrated steel plant, commissioned in 1959. The Bhilai Steel Plant was set up with Soviet assistance. The plant was a fruit of Indo-German friendship, with machinery and expertise provided by Germany’s Krupp and Demag. Options (b), (c) and (d) are correct:
- Salem Steel Plant, located in Tamil Nadu, is a premier producer of stainless steel in India. It’s a unit of SAIL and specializes in producing stainless steel in various forms like coils, sheets, and blades. It caters to both domestic and international markets.
- Maharashtra Elektrosmelt Ltd. (MEL) is a subsidiary of SAIL located in Chandrapur, Maharashtra. It specializes in producing ferroalloys, which are critical raw materials for steel production. Ferroalloys are used to enhance the strength and durability of steel,
- Visakhapatnam Steel Plant also known as Vizag Steel is operated by Rashtriya Ispat Nigam Limited (RINL). It was the first shore-based steel plant in India and plays a key role in regional industrial development.

subject: Economy
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. Sensex is based on 50 of the most important stocks available on the Bombay stock Exchange (BSE). 2. For calculating the Sensex, all the stocks are assigned proportional weightage. 3. The New York Stock Exchange is the oldest stock exchange in the world. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: The BSE Sensex (S&P BSE Sensex) is based on 30 of the most actively traded and financially strong stocks on the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE), not 50. Statement 2 is incorrect: In Sensex calculation, stocks are assigned weightage based on free-float market capitalization, not proportional weightage. This means stocks with higher market capitalization and more freely tradable shares have a greater impact on the index. Statement 3 is incorrect: The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) is one of the largest stock exchanges but not the oldest. The Amsterdam Stock Exchange (Netherlands), established in 1602, is considered the world’s oldest stock exchange. As per Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) reports, the Sensex is widely used as a benchmark index for tracking the performance of the Indian equity market.

solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Poverty Reduction and Growth Facility(PRGF) was actually established by the International Monetary Fund (IMF), not the International Development Association (IDA). subject: Environment
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. 2. Kyoto protocol came into force in the year 2005. Kyoto protocol deals primarily with the depletion of the ozone layer. 3. Methane as a greenhouse gas is more harmful than carbon dioxide. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.detail: The Kyoto Protocol is an international treaty that commits industrialized nations to reducing greenhouse gas emissions in order to mitigate climate change and was adopted at COP 3 (the third Conference of the Parties) in 1997 in Kyoto, Japan. Statement 1 is correct: The Kyoto Protocol officially came into force on February 16, 2005. Statement 2 is incorrect: The Kyoto Protocol primarily focuses on reducing greenhouse gas emissions to combat climate change, not on the depletion of the ozone layer. The ozone layer issue is addressed by the Montreal Protocol, which focuses on phasing out substances that deplete the ozone layer. Statement 3 is correct: Methane (CH₄) is a more potent greenhouse gas than carbon dioxide (CO₂) in the short term. Although CO₂ persists longer in the atmosphere, methane has a much higher global warming potential over a 20-year period—about 84 times more potent than CO₂. 5. CLIMATE CHANGE

subject: Geography
stem: Consider the following statements : 1. The axis of the earth’s magnetic field is inclined at 23 and half to the geographic axis of the earth. 2. The earth’s magnetic pole in the Northern Hemisphere is located on a Peninsula in Northern Canada. 3. The earth’s magnetic equator passes through Thumba in South India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Earth’s magnetic axis is tilted at approximately 11 degrees relative to its geographic axis (the axis around which the Earth rotates). The 23.5-degree tilt refers to the Earth’s rotational axis relative to its orbit around the Sun, which is responsible for the seasons, not the magnetic field. Statement 2 is correct: The North Magnetic Pole is currently located in the Arctic region of Northern Canada, specifically on Ellesmere Island. The magnetic poles are not fixed and move over time due to changes in the Earth’s magnetic field. Statement 3 is correct: The magnetic equator is an imaginary line where the Earth’s magnetic field is entirely horizontal (no dip angle). The magnetic equator passes near Thumba in South India. The Thumba Equatorial Rocket Launching Station was established in this location due to its proximity to the magnetic equator.

subject: Geography
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. Total land area of earth is approximately 1475 lakh square kilometers. 2. Ratio of land area to water area of earth is approximately 1 : 4. 3. Maximum Percentage of earth’s water is in the Pacific Ocean. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: The total land area of the Earth is approximately 1475 lakh square kilometers or 147.5 million square kilometers. The Earth’s surface area is about 510 million square kilometers, with land covering roughly 29% of it. Statement 2 is incorrect: About 71% of the Earth’s surface is covered by water and 29% is land. This gives a land-to-water ratio of approximately 1:2.4 and not 1:4 as mentioned in the question. Statement 3 is correct: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean, covering an area of about 168 million square kilometers. It contains the maximum percentage of Earth’s water, contributing significantly to the planet’s hydrosphere.

subject: Geography
stem: Which one of the following statements is not correct ?
options: There is no definition of the Scheduled Tribe in the constitutions of India.
North-East India accounts for a little over half of the country’s tribal population.
The people known as Todas live in the Nilgiri area.
Lotha is a language spoken in Nagaland.

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: North-East India accounts for a little over half of the country’s tribal population.
solution.detail:
While North-East India has a high proportion of tribal communities relative to its total population, it does not account for over half of India’s total tribal population.The largest tribal populations are actually concentrated in central India—states like Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Maharashtra, Rajasthan, and Jharkhand. As per Census 2011, the North-Eastern states account for approximately 12-15% of the total Scheduled Tribe population in India, not more than half. Statements (a), (c) and (d) are correct:
- The Constitution of India does not provide a specific definition for Scheduled Tribes. Instead, under Article 342, the President is empowered to specify the tribes or tribal communities to be recognized as Scheduled Tribes in consultation with the respective state governments.
- The Todas are an indigenous tribe residing in the Nilgiri Hills of Tamil Nadu. They are known for their distinct culture, traditional barrel-vaulted houses, and pastoral lifestyle centered around buffalo herding.
- Lotha is both a language and the name of a tribe in Nagaland. The Lotha Naga people predominantly inhabit the Wokha district of Nagaland.

stem: Consider the following statements : 1. India is the second country in the world to adopt a National Family Planning Programme. 2. The National Population Policy of India 2000 seeks to achieve a replacement level of fertility by 2010 with a population of 111 crores. 3. Kerala is the first state in India to achieve a replacement level of fertility. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.detail:
Statement 1 is incorrect: India was the first country to launch a National Programme for Family Planning in 1952. The Mission Parivar Vikas, one of the key flagship Family Planning Programmes of Government of India was launched in 2016 for increasing access to contraceptives and family planning services. Statement 2 and 3 are correct:

- The Total Fertility Rate (TFR) represents the average number of children a woman is expected to have during her reproductive years, based on current fertility rates. Kerala was the First State in India to achieve replacement level fertility. The Global Burden of Diseases (GBD) 2021 study notes that India’s TFR was 1.9 in 2021, below the replacement level of 2.1.

subject: Geography
stem: Which one of the following statements is true on the basis of Census 2001 ?
options: Bihar has the highest percentage of the scheduled castes of its population.
The decadal growth of population of India (1991-2001) has been below 20%.
Mizoram is the Indian state with the least population.
Pondicherry has the highest sex ratio among the Union Territories.

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Pondicherry has the highest sex ratio among the Union Territories.
solution.detail: Uttar Pradesh had the largest absolute number of Scheduled Caste (SC) population in both the 2001 and 2011 censuses. However, in terms of the percentage of SC population relative to the total population, Punjab (not Bihar) recorded the highest proportion in both years. In 2001, Punjab’s SC population constituted approximately 28.85% of its total population, which increased to around 31.94% in 2011. In comparison, Uttar Pradesh’s SC population percentage was 21.15% in 2001 and slightly declined to 21.1% in 2011. Option (b) is incorrect: The percentage decadal growth rate of the country has declined significantly from 21.5% for the period 1991-2001 to 17.6 % during 2001-2011. Option (c) is incorrect: Sikkim was the state with the least population (approximately 5.4 lac) in 2001 as well as in 2011 (approximately 6.07 lac). Option (d) is correct: The sex ratio represents the number of females per 1,000 males in a population. Pondicherry (now Puducherry) had a higher proportion of females compared to males(1001 females over 1000 male), resulting in the highest sex ratio among Union Territories, according to the Census 2001 data.

stem: According to the census 2001, which one of the following Indian States has the maximum population in India after Uttar Pradesh?

Reason (R) is false: The western coast (Konkan, Malabar) receives higher rainfall than the eastern coast due to the southwest monsoon and orographic effect of the Western Ghats. The eastern coast receives sufficient rainfall, mainly from the southwest monsoon (except Tamil Nadu, which depends on the northeast monsoon). Additionally, major rivers like the Ganga, Godavari, Krishna, and Kaveri provide irrigation, supporting rice cultivation.

subject: Geography
stem: Which one of the following statements is not correct ?
options: The Western Ghats are relatively lower in their northern region.
The Anai Mudi is the highest peak in the Western Ghats.
Tapi river lies to the south of Satpura
The Narmada and Tapti river valleys are said to be old rift valleys. 280. Gandhi Sagar Dam is a part of which one of the following ? (2005)

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Tapi river lies to the south of Satpura
solution.detail:
The arrangement of these hill ranges in India, from north to south, follows a specific order. Beginning in the north are the Nallamalai Hills, located primarily in Andhra Pradesh. Moving southward, the Javadi Hills, situated in Tamil Nadu, come next. Further south lies the prominent Nilgiri Hills, also in Tamil Nadu, known for their biodiversity and hill stations. Finally, the southernmost range in this sequence is the Anamalai Hills, straddling the border of Kerala and Tamil Nadu, and home to the highest peak in the Western Ghats, Anamudi. Therefore, the correct north-to-south sequence is Nallamalai Hills, followed by the Javadi Hills, then the Nilgiri Hills, and finally the Anamalai Hills.
- Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve is India’s first biosphere reserve (1986), Nilgiri spans 5,520 sq. km across Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and Karnataka. It houses diverse ecosystems like shola forests and grasslands, supports 3,300 plant species (132 endemic), and endangered fauna like the Nilgiri Tahr and Lion-tailed Macaque. Protected areas include Bandipur, Mudumalai, and Silent Valley National Parks.
- Pachmarhi Biosphere Reserve was established in 1999 in Madhya Pradesh’s Satpura Range, Pachmarhi covers 4,926 sq. km. It includes Satpura National Park (core zone), Bori Sanctuary, and Pachmarhi Sanctuary. Known for teak and sal forests, it supports rich biodiversity with tigers, leopards, Indian giant squirrels, and over 300 bird species. UNESCO recognized it in 2009.
solution.extraEdge:
The Nallamalai Hills, a part of the Eastern Ghats, extend across Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, stretching from the Krishna River to the Pennar River. These hills are home to the Nagarjunasagar-Srisailam Tiger Reserve, India’s largest tiger reserve, which plays a crucial role in wildlife conservation.
- The Javadi Hills, a north-south hill range in Tamil Nadu, are a continuation of the Eastern Ghats. Known for their blue granite deposits, sandalwood, and medicinal plants, these hills also support tribal settlements, making them an ecologically and culturally significant region.
- Nilgiri Hills located at the junction of the Eastern and Western Ghats, the Nilgiri Hills span Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and Karnataka. The region is famous for Ooty (Udhagamandalam), tea plantations, and the Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve, which hosts a rich variety of flora and fauna.
- The Anamalai Hills, a southern extension of the Western Ghats, stretch across Tamil Nadu and Kerala. They are home to the Anamalai Tiger Reserve and vast tea, coffee, and spice plantations, making them an important biodiversity hotspot and agricultural zone.

subject: Geography
stem: Which one of the following is the correct sequence of hills starting from the north and going towards the south ?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Nallamalai Hills–Javadi Hills–Nilgiri Hills –Anamalai Hills
solution.detail:
Assertion (A) is true: The west-flowing rivers of Peninsular India, such as the Narmada, Tapi, Mahi, and Mandovi, generally do not form deltas due to the following reasons:
- Steep Gradient and Short Course: These rivers flow through steep slopes of the Western Ghats and have a short course before reaching the Arabian Sea. This rapid flow prevents sediment deposition.
- Narrow Continental Shelf: The western coast has a narrow continental shelf, causing sediments to be quickly carried into deeper waters rather than accumulating near the mouth to form deltas.
- High Energy Waves and Currents: The Arabian Sea experiences strong wave action and currents that disperse sediments away from river mouths, further inhibiting delta formation. Reason (R) is false: While some west-flowing rivers might carry less alluvial sediment compared to east-flowing rivers that traverse vast plains, it’s not accurate to say they carry no alluvial sediments. The absence of deltas is due to the factors like the steep gradient of the rivers, the nature of the terrain they flow through, and the lack of a significant continental shelf where sediment could be deposited to form a delta. Thus, Assertion (A) is true and Reason (R) is false. 292. Assertion (A): Bangalore receives much higher average annual rainfall than that of Mangalore. Reason (R): Bangalore has the benefit of receiving rainfall both from south-west and north-east monsoons. (2004) (a) Both A and R are individually true and R is the correct explanation of A (b) Both A and R are individually true but R is not the correct explanation of A (c) A is true but R is false (d) A is false but R is true

subject: Geography
stem: Which one of the following is not a Biosphere reserve ?

solution.detail: Biosphere Reserves are areas of terrestrial and coastal ecosystems promoting solutions to reconcile the conservation of biodiversity with its sustainable use. They are designated by UNESCO under its Man and Biosphere (MAB) Programme. Currently, India has 18 Biosphere Reserves, designated to conserve biodiversity, ecosystems, and traditional lifestyles. Option (b) is correct: Agasthyamalai, Nilgiri, and Panchmarhi are all recognized Biosphere Reserves in India. The Nallamalai Hills, while a significant geographical feature, are not currently designated as a Biosphere Reserve.

optionNotes.text: Agasthyamalai, Nilgiri, and Panchmarhi are all recognized Biosphere Reserves in India. The Nallamalai Hills, while a significant geographical feature, are not currently designated as a Biosphere Reserve.

solution.extraEdge: Agasthyamala Biosphere Reserve is located in the southern Western Ghats, Agasthyamala Biosphere Reserve spans 3,500 sq. km across Kerala and Tamil Nadu. Recognized by UNESCO in 2016, it harbors tropical forests, 2,254 plant species (405 endemic), and rare fauna like the Nilgiri Tahr and Asian Elephant. It includes Neyyar, Peppara, and Shendurney Wildlife Sanctuaries and Kalakad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve.

subject: Geography
stem: Which one of the following is the correct sequence of Indian cities in the decreasing order of their normal annual rainfall ?

solution.detail: Kochi receives the highest rainfall (~3000 mm) due to the Southwest Monsoon and its proximity to the Western Ghats, making it one of the wettest cities in India. Kolkata follows with around 1600 mm of rainfall, heavily influenced by the Bay of Bengal branch of the monsoon, with most rain occurring between June and September. Patna, with approximately 1200 mm of annual rainfall, experiences moderate monsoon rains, though lesser than Kolkata. Delhi, receiving around 800 mm of rainfall, has a semi-arid climate, with monsoon rains but significantly lower precipitation than the other cities. Thus, the correct sequence in decreasing order of rainfall is Kochi–Kolkata–Patna–Delhi.

subject: Geography
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. 2. Silent Valley National Park is in the Nallamalai range. Pathrakkadavu Hydroelectric project is proposed to be built near the Silent Valley National Park. 3. The Kunthi river originates in Silent Valley’s rainforests. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: Silent Valley National Park is part of the Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve and is located in Kerala’s Western Ghats. It is not situated in the Nallamalai range. The park is known for its untouched tropical rainforest, rich biodiversity, and ecological importance. It is home to rare species like the lion-tailed macaque, Nilgiri langur, and Malabar giant squirrel. The park also has 960 plant species and 170 bird species. In the 1970s, the “Save Silent Valley” movement helped protect it. This movement stopped a hydroelectric project that threatened the park’s ecosystem. Silent Valley was declared a national park in 1984. Statement 2 is correct: The Pathrakkadavu Hydro Electric Project was proposed near the Silent Valley National Park. This proposal sparked significant environmental concerns and debate due to the potential impact on the park’s fragile ecosystem. Statement 3 is correct: The Kunthi River, originating from the Anginda peak in the Silent Valley National Park, Kerala, is a tributary of the Bharathapuzha River. Known for its perennial flow due to its source in dense tropical rainforests, the river supports biodiversity and local communities. The river is undammed so far, preserving its natural ecosystem.

The park is known for its untouched tropical rainforest, rich biodiversity, and ecological importance. It is home to rare species like the lion-tailed macaque, Nilgiri langur, and Malabar giant squirrel. The park also has 960 plant species and 170 bird species. In the 1970s, the “Save Silent Valley” movement helped protect it. This movement stopped a hydroelectric project that threatened the park’s ecosystem. Silent Valley was declared a national park in 1984.

The Pathrakkadavu Hydro Electric Project was proposed near the Silent Valley National Park. This proposal sparked significant environmental concerns and debate due to the potential impact on the park’s fragile ecosystem.

The Kunthi River, originating from the Anginda peak in the Silent Valley National Park, Kerala, is a tributary of the Bharathapuzha River. Known for its perennial flow due to its source in dense tropical rainforests, the river supports biodiversity and local communities. The river is undammed so far, preserving its natural ecosystem.

stem: Itaipu Dam built on the river Parana is one of the largest dams in the world. Which of the following two countries have this as a joint project ?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Brazil and Paraguay
solution.detail: The Itaipu Dam, located on the Parana River at the border of Foz do Iguaçu (Brazil) and Ciudad del Este (Paraguay), is one of the largest hydroelectric plants in the world. Completed in 1984, it is managed by Itaipu Binacional, equally owned by Brazil and Paraguay. The dam generates over 14,000 MW of electricity, supplying nearly 90% of Paraguay’s energy needs and about 15% of Brazil’s consumption. It remains a symbol of successful binational cooperation and a major contributor to renewable energy production globally.

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: The Biosafety protocol deals with genetically modified organisms.
solution.detail:
Statement (c) is correct: The Cartagena Protocol on Biosafety is an international agreement aimed at regulating the safe transfer, handling, and use of living modified organisms (LMOs), or genetically modified organisms (GMOs), to protect biodiversity and human health. Statements (a), (b) and (d) are incorrect:
- The first Meeting of the Parties (MOP 1) to the Cartagena Protocol on Biosafety was held in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, from February 23–27, 2004, not in the Philippines.
- India is a signatory to both the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) and the Cartagena Protocol on Biosafety. It ratified the protocol on January 23, 2003, and actively participates in its implementation.
- The United States is not a party to the Cartagena Protocol on Biosafety, as it has not ratified the agreement.

subject: Modern History
stem: Which one of the following is the correct statement?
options: The modern Kochi was a Dutch colony till India’s independence.
The Dutch defeated the Portuguese and built Fort Williams in modern Kochi.
Modern Kochi was first a Dutch colony before the Portuguese took over.
The modern Kochi never became a part of a British colony.

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: The modern Kochi never became a part of a British colony.
solution.detail: Kochi, located on the Malabar Coast, was a key maritime hub during the colonial era due to its strategic location and spice trade. Over centuries, Kochi witnessed control by multiple European powers, including the Portuguese, Dutch, and British, each leaving a distinct mark on its history and architecture. Statement (a) is incorrect: The Dutch controlled Kochi after defeating the Portuguese in 1663, but their control ended long before India’s independence. Kochi later became a princely state under British suzerainty. Statement (b) is incorrect: The Portuguese built Fort Emmanuel in Kochi, not Fort William. Fort William, located in Kolkata, was constructed by the British. Statement (c) is incorrect: The Portuguese were the first Europeans to establish control over Kochi in 1503. The Dutch defeated the Portuguese in 1663 and took over, but Kochi was never a Dutch colony before the Portuguese. Statement (d) is correct: Kochi remained a princely state under British indirect rule, but it was never fully integrated as a part of British India. The local rulers retained nominal control, and Kochi maintained its identity distinct from direct British colonies.

subject: Modern History
stem: Which one of the following is the correct chronological order of the battles fought in India in the 18th Century?
options: Battle of Wandiwash–Battle of Buxar–Battle of Ambur–Battle of Plassey
Battle of Ambur–Battle of Plassey–Battle of Wandiwash–Battle of Buxar
Battle of Wandiwash–Battle of Plassey–Battle of Ambur–Battle of Buxar
Battle of Ambur–Battle of Buxar–Battle of Wandiwash–Battle of Plassey

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Battle of Ambur–Battle of Plassey–Battle of Wandiwash–Battle of Buxar
solution.detail:
The 18th century in India was a tumultuous period marked by battles involving European powers and Indian states, vying for dominance over trade, territory, and political control. Key battles during this era shaped the trajectory of British colonial rule and defined European influence in India.
- The Battle of Ambur (1749), during the Second Carnatic War, marked the start of Anglo-French rivalry, with the French backing Chanda Sahib and the British supporting Muhammad Ali.
- The Battle of Plassey (1757) saw Robert Clive’s British East India Company defeat Siraj-ud-Daulah, establishing British political control in India.
- The Battle of Wandiwash (1760), in the Third Carnatic War, ended French ambitions in India with a British victory.
- The Battle of Buxar (1764) secured British control over Bengal, Bihar, and Odisha, defeating a coalition of the Nawab of Awadh, Mughal Emperor Shah Alam II, and Mir Qasim.

subject: Modern History
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. Warren Hastings was the first Governor General who established a regular police force in India on the British pattern. 2. A Supreme Court was established at Calcutta by the Regulating Act, 1773. 3. The Indian Penal Code came into effect in the year 1860. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: Warren Hastings, the first Governor-General of India, did not establish a regular police force. Instead, he reorganized the existing system of policing and appointed a superintendent of police for each district. The British-style police system was introduced later by Lord Cornwallis in 1786. Statement 2 is correct: The Regulating Act of 1773 established the Supreme Court in Calcutta with jurisdiction over Bengal, Bihar, and Orissa, hearing civil, criminal, and commercial cases, and exercising both original and appellate jurisdiction. Statement 3 is correct: The Indian Penal Code (IPC) was drafted by the first Law Commission of India established in 1834 under the Charter Act of 1833 under the chairmanship Thomas Babington Macaulay and was formally enacted in 1860 during the time of the British East India Company.

Instead, he reorganized the existing system of policing and appointed a superintendent of police for each district. The British-style police system was introduced later by Lord Cornwallis in 1786.

subject: Modern History

solution.detail: The Preamble to the Constitution of India is part of the Constitution but lacks independent legal force. It outlines guiding principles like justice, liberty, and equality. The Supreme Court in Keshavananda Bharati (1973) affirmed it as integral to understanding the Constitution’s philosophy, although it doesn’t confer enforceable rights. It serves as a guiding tool for interpreting the Constitution’s objectives. Schedule Added by Amendment Purpose
solution.extraEdge:
In 1976, Preamble was amended by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act to add "Socialist, Secular, and Integrity".
- In the Berubari Union Case (1960), the Supreme Court had earlier held that the Preamble is not a source of power or substantive rights and has no independent legal effect. It also held that Preamble is not an operative part of the Constitution and lacks legal force. Ninth Schedule First Amendment Act (1951) Protects certain laws (mainly land reforms) from judicial review. Tenth Schedule Fifty-Second Amendment Act (1985) Deals with disqualification of legislators on grounds of defection (Anti-Defection Law). Eleventh Schedule Seventy-Third Amendment Act (1992) Defines powers, authority, and responsibilities of Panchayati Raj institutions. Twelfth Schedule Seventy-Fourth Amendment Act (1992) Lists functions and responsibilities of municipalities.

subject: Polity
stem: The Constitution (98th Amendment) Bill is related to:
options: empowering the centre to levy and appropriate service tax
the Constitution of the National Judicial Commission
readjustment of electoral constituencies on the basis of the population census 2001
the demarcation of new boundaries between States.

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: the Constitution of the National Judicial Commission
solution.detail: The 98th Constitutional Amendment Bill was introduced in India to reform the judicial appointment process by establishing a National Judicial Commission (NJC). The NJC was proposed to replace the existing collegium system, which had been criticized for its lack of transparency and accountability in selecting judges. The Commission would have included the Chief Justice of India, senior judges, and members from the executive, ensuring a more inclusive and structured process for judicial appointments. However, the proposed amendment did not come into effect, as it was struck down by the Supreme Court, which upheld the collegium system in its 2015 judgment. Probably UPSC has mistakenly used word ‘Act instead of bill’.

subject: Polity
stem: Consider the following: 1. Disputes with mobile cellular companies 2. Motor accident cases 3. Pension cases For which of the above are Lok Adalats held?

solution.detail:
Lok Adalats. established under the Legal Services Authorities Act, 1987, are a key mechanism for resolving disputes through amicable settlements.
- They address a broad spectrum of cases, including consumer disputes, such as those with mobile cellular companies, which are commonly resolved due to their potential for quick compromise.
- Additionally, Lok Adalats frequently handle motor accident compensation claims under the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988, as they involve monetary settlements that can be resolved without prolonged litigation.
- Pension-related disputes, often involving retired government employees, are also taken up to ensure timely resolution and delivery of justice to senior citizens. These cases, being non-complex in nature and suitable for settlement, are ideally suited for Lok Adalats, which aim to reduce the burden on regular courts while providing cost-effective and speedy justice to litigants.

The Constitution of India and the United States of America envisage a dual polity (The Union and the States) but a single citizenship. 3. A naturalized citizen of India can never be deprived of his citizenship. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.detail: Articles 371A to 371I were added through various amendments to preserve the cultural, social, and economic interests of specific regions, addressing their unique needs. Statement 1 is correct: Articles 371A to 371I were added to meet regional demands of states like Nagaland, Assam, Manipur, Andhra Pradesh, Sikkim, Mizoram, Arunachal Pradesh, and Goa. Statement 2 is incorrect: Although both constitutions allow a dual system of governance, the United States permits dual citizenship, while India adheres to single citizenship. Statement 3 is also incorrect: In India, a naturalized citizen can lose citizenship if acquired through fraud or disloyalty to the Constitution.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Which one of the following is the landmark performance of Dr V. Mohan Reddy, an Indian, born doctor working in the U.S.A. during February, 2005?
options: Research on human stem cells which is likely to revolutionise treatment of deadly diseases like cancer.
Successful open heart procedure called arterial switch performed on an infant.
Research on genetic engineering which can help in treatment of deadly diseases like AIDS.
Successful brain surgery to revive the function of the nearly dead brain of a premature born infant.

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Successful open heart procedure called arterial switch performed on an infant.
solution.detail: Dr. V. Mohan Reddy is renowned for performing a successful open-heart procedure called the arterial switch operation on an infant. This operation is a complex cardiac surgery used to correct transposition of the great arteries, a congenital heart defect where the major arteries are reversed. This breakthrough surgery provided new hope for treating congenital heart diseases in infants and gained recognition as a landmark achievement in pediatric cardiac surgery.

stem: What is the purpose of the US Space Agency’s Themis Mission, which was recently in the news?

To study the satellites of Saturn
To study the colourful of display of high latitude skies
To build a space laboratory to study the stellar explosions

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: To study the colourful of display of high latitude skies
solution.detail: NASA’s THEMIS mission (launched in 2007) investigates the origin and mechanisms of auroras, the colorful lights in high-latitude skies caused by interactions between Earth’s magnetosphere and solar wind. The mission studies substorms—sudden energy releases in the magnetosphere—and processes like magnetic reconnection and plasma waves. that magnetotail reconnection triggers substorms, improving understanding of space weather and its impacts on satellites, communication systems. The mission contributes to heliophysics by exploring Earth’s magnetosphere dynamics. Its primary goal was to study auroras and their connection to energy releases in the magnetosphere.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Which one of the following is the correct sequence of the given substances in the decreasing order of their densities?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Gold > Mercury > Steel
solution.detail:
Density is a physical property that represents the compactness of a substance. It is defined as the mass of a substance per unit volume. The higher the density, the more mass is packed into a given volume.
- Gold is known for its high density. It is a dense metal with a density of approximately 19.3 grams per cubic centimeter (g/cm³). This makes gold denser than both steel and mercury.
- Mercury is a liquid metal at room temperature and has a relatively low density compared to both gold and steel. Its density is approximately 13.6 g/cm³, making it less dense than gold but denser than steel.
- Steel, which is an alloy primarily composed of iron and carbon, has a lower density than gold but is still relatively dense compared to many other materials. The density of steel varies depending on its composition, but it typically ranges from around 7.7 g/cm³ to 8.1 g/cm³.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: What is the order of magnitude of electric resistance of the human body (dry)?

solution.detail: The dry human body has an electric resistance of the order of magnitude of 10^4 ohm (10,000 ohm). This resistance can vary depending on factors such as skin moisture, contact area, and individual characteristics. Dry skin offers a relatively high resistance to the flow of electric current due to its insulating properties. The resistance of the human body plays a significant role in determining the effects of electric shocks and electrical safety measures. Higher resistance limits the current flow through the body, reducing the potential harm caused by electric shock. However, it’s important to note that wet or moist conditions can significantly decrease the body’s resistance and increase the risk of electrical injury. subject: Science and Tech
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. Dengue is a protozoan disease transmitted by mosquitoes. 2. Retro-orbital pain is not a symptom of dengue. 3. Skin rash and bleeding from nose and gums are some of the symptoms of dengue. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: Dengue is not caused by a protozoan but by the dengue virus (DENV), of the Flaviviridae family. The virus spreads through bites from infected Aedes mosquitoes, primarily Aedes aegypti and Aedes albopictus. Statement 2 is incorrect: Retro-orbital pain, which refers to pain behind the eyes, is a common symptom of dengue fever. Patients often experience severe headache accompanied by retro-orbital pain during the febrile phase of the illness. Statement 3 is correct Many dengue patients experience a skin rash, which usually appears a few days after the fever starts. Some people even develop a “white islands on a red sea” rash, where parts of the skin turn white amid the redness. Nosebleeds (epistaxis) and bleeding gums, are common in dengue patients particularly in severe forms like dengue hemorrhagic fever (DHF) or dengue shock syndrome (DSS).

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Which of the following diseases of milching animals are infectious? 1. Foot and mouth disease 2. Anthrax 3. Black Quarter 4. Cowpox Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

solution.detail: Foot and mouth disease (FMD) is a highly infectious viral disease that affects cloven-hoofed animals, including cattle, sheep, goats, and pigs. It is caused by the foot-andmouth disease virus (a member of the Picornaviridae family). The disease spreads rapidly through direct contact, contaminated feed, or aerosols. Anthrax is a severe bacterial disease caused by Bacillus anthracis. It primarily affects cattle, sheep, and goats, but can also infect humans through spores present in contaminated soil, water, or feed. Symptoms include sudden death, high fever, difficulty breathing, and bleeding from body openings. The disease is zoonotic, meaning it can spread from animals to humans. Black Quarter, also known as Blackleg, is an infectious bacterial disease caused by Clostridium chauvoei. It mainly affects young cattle and sheep which results in painful swelling of muscles, fever, lameness, and rapid death due to toxin production. The disease spreads through ingestion of spores present in soil, which activate under favorable conditions. Cowpox is an infectious viral disease caused by the cowpox virus, which belongs to the Orthopoxvirus family. It primarily affects cattle but can also infect humans. subject: Science and Tech
stem: Pneumoconiosis affects the workers who work mainly in

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Coal mining industry
solution.detail: Workers in the coal mining industry are most affected by pneumoconiosis. Pneumoconiosis is a lung disease caused by the inhalation of dust, particularly mineral or industrial dust over a prolonged period. It primarily affects workers in industries where they are exposed to high levels of airborn e particles. Coal workers’ pneumoconiosis (CWP), also known as black lung disease, is caused by prolonged inhalation of coal dust, making coal miners the most affected group. Other forms of pneumoconiosis include silicosis (caused by silica dust, affecting quarry and glass industry workers) and asbestosis (caused by asbestos exposure).
